rulings.Case Title: SCOTT FRIEDMAN VS. MICHAEL J. SPOHN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Costs Pursuant To A Contract
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 20, 2014, Line 1: PLAINTIFF SCOTT FRIEDMAN's Motion For Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Costs: GRANTED. Plaintiff shall recover fees in the amount of $9,012.5 and costs in the amount of $324.43. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND PARKER-GAYLORD VS. DEUTSCHE BANK, NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 20, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY and JP MORGAN CHASE BANK'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT: Continued to September 4, 2014 at 9:30 AM in Dept. 501, on Court's own motion. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE LEE CEARLOCK et al VS. ALYCE D. CRAFT et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 20, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANT ALYCE CRAFT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT: Sustained without leave to amend as to the causes of action for fraud and negligent misrepresentation per Hunter v. Upright (1993) 6 Cal.4th 1174, Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, and Robinson Helicopter v. Dana Corporation (2004) 34 Cal.4th 979, as Plaintiff cannot plead detrimental reliance and damages separate and apart from damages caused by the breach of contract. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE LEE CEARLOCK et al VS. ALYCE D. CRAFT et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 20, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANT ALYCE CRAFT'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT: GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to the request for punitive damages. Motion is other wise denied. Allegations in paragraph 56 and 57 are not improper; whether plaintiff had to comply with or was excused from the contract provisions regarding exercise of the option to renew after defendant's breach of contract, is an issue that cannot be properly addressed at this stage of litigation on a motion to strike. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GAETANI REAL ESTATE, INC. VS. MARCHELLO KARAGIO et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 20, 2014, Line 10: DEFENDANT MARCHELLO KARAGIO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Unlawful detainer cause of action is sufficiently alleged and is not uncertain. Notice specifies the nuisance activity as "banging" and "loud communications" at all hours of night. (501/REQ)
